notes: Major update: exploit path now picks by PnL% not WR (fixes bug where SL-2p0 was exploited over SL-1p5). Kill thresholds tightened: PnL<-15% primary, WR<5% secondary. EPSILON lowered to 15%. Historical data shows: IMMEDIATE (-57%!), TS-0p5-0p3 (-62%!), SL-2p0 (-48%!) should have been killed earlier. Winners: SL-1p5, TS-1p0-0p5, RETRACE-2.

tests.name: sl-distance-test
tests.description: Stop-loss distance. WINNER: SL-1p5 (1.5%). Key insight: 73% of closes happen in <5min — SL distance matters less than avoiding fee-driven micro-stops. Wider SL (1.5%) avoids noise; tighter SLs (0.5%, 1.0%) get fee-whipsawed; 3.0% holds losers too long.

variants.name: Runner-up — SL 1.0%
variants.learnings: Best WR (23.8%) but negative PnL. avg_win=-0.085% (NEGATIVE!) means winners are smaller than losers in absolute terms — fee drag + direction failures. Explore only, not exploit.

variants.name: Loser — SL 3.0% (reduced weight, monitor)
variants.learnings: PnL=-6.8% but above PnL_KILL=-15% so not auto-killed. avg_win negative = losing when right. Holds losers too long. Keep at low weight for more data.

variants.name: WORST — SL 0.5% (minimal weight)
variants.learnings: Too tight — gets stopped by noise. 73% closes <5min makes 0.5% SL essentially random. Minimal weight for data only.

variants.name: EVOLVED: Dynamic 0.8% SL that widens on profit

config.description: Start tight (0.8%), widen to 1.5% once position is +0.5% in profit. Hypothesis: best of both worlds.

variants.learnings: New hypothesis: SL tightens/widens based on position state. Needs trades to evaluate.

variants.name: NEW: SL 1.2% (between 1.0% and 1.5%)
variants.learnings: New: test if 1.2% is the sweet spot. 1.0% gets fee-whipsawed, 1.5% wins. 1.2% might split the difference.

config.description: EVOLVED: wider SL (0.0%) derived from SL-1p0 win=50%

tests.name: trailing-stop-test
tests.description: Trailing stop activation and distance. WINNER: TS-1p0-0p5 (+10.72%). Critical insight: 73% of closes happen in <5min — tight trailing lock-in is essential. 1% activate is enough to catch micro-rallies without being too loose.

variants.name: WINNER — TS 1% activate / 0.5% trail
variants.learnings: CLEAR WINNER. Best PnL across all tests. avg_win=0.387% vs avg_loss=-0.127% = 3:1 reward/risk. Best combo: SL-1p5 + TS-1p0-0p5.

variants.name: Marginal — TS 2% activate / 1% trail
variants.learnings: Best WR (24.4%) but barely profitable (PnL=0.31%). 2% activation too high — by the time price moves 2%, momentum often reverses. Explore weight kept for more data.

variants.name: Loser — TS 1.5% activate / 0.8% trail
variants.learnings: Both too wide. avg_loss=-0.626% means it holds losers too long before trailing kicks in. Low weight for data.

variants.name: EVOLVED: TS 0.5% activate / 0.4% trail

config.description: Even tighter than winner. Hypothesis: in choppy market with 73% closes <5min, 0.5% activate catches micro-rallies faster.

variants.learnings: Hypothesis: tighter than winner might win even more. Risk: activates on noise. Low weight explore.

variants.name: CONTROL: No trailing stop

config.description: No trailing stop. Pure SL/TP management. Tests if TS adds value vs. pure SL.

variants.learnings: Control to measure TS value. Does trailing actually improve PnL, or is it just noise?

tests.name: entry-timing-test
tests.description: Entry timing. WINNER: RETRACE-2 (+3.99%). Fast entry (0.2% max wait) beats waiting for pullbacks. Market doesn't give second chances — enter when signal fires.

variants.name: WINNER — Fast entry (0.2% wait / 30min)
variants.learnings: WINNER despite lower WR. avg_win=0.974% vs avg_loss=-0.212% = 4.6:1 reward/risk. Fast entry catches moves before reversals. EXPLOIT.

variants.name: Loser — Slow entry (0.5% wait / 60min)
variants.learnings: Waiting for bigger pullbacks loses. avg_win negative = even winners are losing after fees. Reduce weight.

variants.name: Pullback + confirmation filter

config.description: Wait 0.5% pullback but require RSI/momentum confirmation. Tests if RETRACE-5 fails due to lack of filter.

variants.learnings: Hypothesis: RETRACE-5 fails because no confirmation on pullback. Add RSI/momentum filter.
